“I started by asking if you could prove your game is fair, and the honest answer was three words: ‘we think so.’” [point to the headline] “The whole point of today was to turn that into ‘we can prove it’ — and to know exactly how high you need to climb to get there.”

“But if you take one thing home, make it this:” [read the lede] “the techniques aren’t the point — the calibration is. Know what each signal actually proves. Build the rung your stakes demand — and no higher. Attach a reason to everything. And then stop.”

[gesture to the QR] “Everything you saw — the worksheet, the runnable rungs, the AWS architectures, every live demo — is one git clone away. Go climb it on your own game. Thank you.”

[Reminder: swap the [QR] placeholder for the real repo link before presenting.]

Close. Return to the opening promise, hand off to action. Short; energy = "go do this," not "in summary."

Headline. Bookend — echoes "we think so" from slide 3, pays off the title. The arc closes.

Lede — "the techniques aren't the point, the calibration is." The one sentence to leave them with. De-emphasizes the (mostly not novel) tech, elevates the judgment: know what each signal proves, build to your stakes, attach a reason, stop. Four verbs, the whole talk.

QR + kit. Replace [QR] with the real repo link. End on the kit — the last image is the thing they take with them. Mention every live demo they poked is in the repo.

67/67·Appendix · credits & sources
Appendix · standing on shoulders — every technique is public, credited & linked

Credits, references & further reading — by rung

R1 Detect · Isolation ForestLiu, Ting & Zhou 2008
Local Outlier FactorBreunig et al. 2000
Robust z / MADLeys et al. 2013
Node2Vec · GraphSAGE · GAT1607.00653 · 1706.02216 · 1710.10903
SHAPLundberg & Lee 2017
LLM moderation (deployed)Ubisoft, KDD 2025
Input-trace transformer (anti-cheat)AntiCheatPT 2025
R2 Quantify · conformal predictionAngelopoulos & Bates · Vovk et al. 2005
Skill rating (open)Glicko-2 · OpenSkill
R3 Attribute · synthetic controlAbadie & Gardeazabal 2003
CUPED · causal forestsDeng et al. 2013 · Wager & Athey
R4 Stress-test · RL playtestingEA SEED, IEEE CoG 2020
Self-play (survey)2408.01072
Economy simulation (MMO)NetEase Fuxi, KDD 2025
R5 Verify rules · Z3 · cvc5Z3 (MSR) · cvc5
TLA+ · Alloy · QuickCheckTLA+ · Alloy · QuickCheck 2000
AWS Bedrock Automated Reasoning checksdocs.aws.amazon.com
AWS provable security (Zelkova/Dafny/s2n-tls)amazon.science
R6 Verify pipeline · SHA-256NIST FIPS 180-4
VRF · Merkle treeRFC 9381 (VRF) · Merkle 1979
Nitro Enclaves attestationdocs.aws.amazon.com
R7 Expose · provably-fair patternopen crypto-gaming convention (commit-reveal, above)
Cross-cut · drift (PSI / KS / ADWIN)PSI · ADWIN (Bifet & Gavaldà 2007)
Cross-cut · fairness auditingBarocas, Hardt & Narayanan · Equalised odds (Hardt et al.)
Evidence-strength ladder (the framing’s lineage)GRADE, BMJ 2008

The contribution of this talk is the synthesis and framing — a seven-rung Confidence Ladder for game integrity — not the underlying methods, which are all public, standard, and linked above. Anti-cheat systems named (Valve Trust Factor, Activision RICOCHET, Riot Vanguard) and studio examples (EA, DeepMind, NetEase) are described from public information only, as illustration — not as methods claimed here.

▶ Speaker & reader notes
▶ Say this (only if asked / for the posted deck)

“I won’t read this live, but the posted deck carries it: every technique on every rung is a standard, public method, here with a direct link to the primary source. My job today was only to put them on one ladder and point it at game integrity.”

Appendix — credits with LINKS, by rung. Every reference is a real clickable link (arXiv / DOI / official docs) — opens in-browser when online; offline it's still a visible, accurate citation. Organised by rung so anyone can trace any claim. Honours the close slide's "credits, with links, in the appendix."

Link hygiene. Used stable identifiers (DOI, arXiv IDs, RFC, FIPS, official AWS docs, project homepages) rather than fragile blog URLs. Re-verify before submission — AWS doc paths and a couple of arXiv IDs can move. The EA SEED arXiv id and a few others were captured from research; spot-check.

Why this matters. Protects against both "you reinvented X" and "you claimed Y as your own." Showing linked lineage makes the synthesis more credible, not less.

— end · 67 slides · generated from confidence-ladder-deck-v3.html —
